summary: @wagni_bot/bsc-sdk postinstall exfiltrates SSH keys,.env, and secret env vars to hardcoded IP
details: This package is a credential-stealing typosquat impersonating a BSC (Binance Smart Chain) SDK. index.js exports an empty object; the package's entire runtime is its postinstall lifecycle script. On `npm install`, postinstall.js walks process.cwd() and the user's home directory (including ~/.ssh) looking for files matching id_rsa, id_ed25519, *.pem, *.key, *.cred, and.env, reads their contents, and POSTs them to http://107.161.90.180:7777 over plain HTTP. It also filters process.env for keys matching PRIVATE/SECRET/TOKEN/API_KEY/PASSWORD/MNEMONIC/SEED/WALLET/AWS and uploads those values. Each POST is tagged with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, and process.cwd() so the attacker can attribute stolen material to a specific victim. The name '@wagni_bot/bsc-sdk' plus the 'Unofficial bsc-sdk SDK' description indicates a lure squatting on the BSC ecosystem; there is no functional SDK code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
